mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes that is designed for ease of deployment and management using Juju. It is ideal for users who want a fully managed Kubernetes experience on various cloud platforms while leveraging the latest features of Kubernetes. Key trade-offs include the need for Juju and the potential complexity of managing charms.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
Juju is required to deploy Charmed Kubernetes. This step installs the Juju client on your local machine.

```bash

sudo snap install juju --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of Juju.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Juju Controller
steps.description:
This command initializes a Juju controller, which is necessary for managing your Kubernetes deployment.

```bash

juju bootstrap aws/us-east-1 --config bootstrap-series=jammy

```

> Replace 'aws/us-east-1' with your desired cloud and region.

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Charmed Kubernetes bundle, which includes all necessary components.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes

```

> This will deploy the latest version of Charmed Kubernetes.

steps.title: Verify Deployment
steps.description:
After deployment, verify that all Kubernetes components are running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Check the status of the deployed services.

steps.title: Configure Kubernetes Access
steps.description:
Set up your kubeconfig to access the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

juju config kubernetes-master kubeconfig

```

> This retrieves the kubeconfig for accessing your cluster.

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This will remove the Charmed Kubernetes deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

juju destroy-controller --destroy-all-models --yes

```

> This command will remove the Juju controller and all models.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Ensure that any orphaned resources such as load balancers or persistent volumes are removed.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-load-balancers | jq '.LoadBalancers[] | .LoadBalancerName'

```

> List load balancers to check for any that need manual deletion.

upgrade.title: Backup Current Deployment
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current Juju models and configurations.

```bash

juju export-model > model-backup.yaml

```

> This command exports the current model configuration.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
This command upgrades the Charmed Kubernetes deployment to the latest version.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes --revision <new-revision>

```

> Replace <new-revision> with the desired version.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the deployment to ensure the upgrade was successful.

```bash

juju status

```

> Verify that all services are running as expected after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju bootstrap failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the cloud provider credentials are incorrect or insufficient.

**Fix:**
Verify your cloud provider credentials and permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubernetes-master not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes master may not have completed its initialization.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju status' to check logs and ensure all services are up.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju deploy failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if there are network issues or if the charm is not available.

**Fix:**
Check your network connectivity and ensure the charm is available on Charmhub.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeconfig not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not have been generated or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju config kubernetes-master kubeconfig' to regenerate the kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31 features, including new API resources and improved metrics.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure all charms are compatible with v1.31.

resolution.summary: You should see 'kubernetes-master' and other components in the 'active' state when running 'juju status'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Juju installed and a compatible cloud environment set up.
